agent_outputs.raw: Defense wins. English-only evaluation for an English-only deployment is methodologically appropriate. Pre-specified deployment restriction, future-month test set from the English deployment population, fairness appendix with disclosure of unobserved dimensions. Absence of evidence for non-English contexts is not evidence against the English-only claim. VERDICT: defense_wins

The evaluation scope matches the deployment scope. Non-English messages are excluded from the model by routing policy. The future-month test set represents the English-only deployment population. Fairness limitations are disclosed. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

The key insight is limitation versus invalidation. The English-only evaluation correctly characterizes what the model does for the population it is deployed on. Out-of-scope gaps are disclosed. The evaluation is valid. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: All three assessors unanimously find the evaluation valid. The evaluation scope matches the deployment scope, limitations are disclosed, and the absence of evidence for non-claimed populations does not invalidate the in-scope result. ETD constraint: unanimous defense wins.
all_issues_raised: English-only scope insufficient for global product — resolved by limitation-versus-invalidation distinction

empirical_test.condition: Verify English-only deployment restriction pre-specified before training; confirm future-month test represents English deployment population; confirm no cross-language generalization claims
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Model applied to non-English messages; or report makes cross-language claims without evidence
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Restriction pre-specified; test set matches deployment scope; no unsupported generalization
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Evaluation correctly scoped but product marketing implies broader language coverage
